symptoms.symptom_name: Three weeks of patchy alopecia with thick greyish scaling
symptoms.character: patchy bald areas with thick greyish-white scale

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patchy scaly alopecia over weeks is a hallmark of tinea capitis and distinguishes it from non-scaling causes of hair loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Black dots — hair shafts broken off flush with the scalp

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The 'black dot' pattern of broken hairs is highly characteristic of endothrix tinea capitis and is visible on video without a clinician.
Alopecia areata gives smooth non-scaly patches without broken black-dot hairs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute boggy, tender, suppurative vertex mass (kerion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A boggy tender suppurative scalp mass superimposed on scaly alopecia is a kerion — video-observable and parent-reportable; the boggy/spongy quality (vs a tense fluctuant cavity) points away from a simple bacterial abscess.
A spongy, multi-pustular mass arising on scaly alopecic patches favours a kerion over a unilocular bacterial abscess, though palpation is needed to be certain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe scalp pain preventing sleep and play

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense pain reflects the severe inflammatory reaction of a kerion and drives the urgency of same-day assessment and analgesia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense scalp itch and follicular pustules around the mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritus and peripheral follicular pustules accompany inflammatory dermatophyte infection; the pustules can prompt (mistaken) consideration of primary bacterial folliculitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, alert, feeding — systemically well

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, lethargy and systemic upset argues against systemic bacterial infection and keeps the disposition at urgent same-day in-person care rather than emergency escalation.
A new puppy is a classic animal source of Microsporum canis; zoophilic species provoke intense inflammation, explaining the kerion and driving contact-precaution and veterinary-check advice.

symptoms.symptom_name: Boggy, non-fluctuant spongy quality of the mass on palpation (no unilocular pus cavity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A spongy mass without the tense fluctuance of a unilocular abscess supports a kerion over a drainable bacterial abscess — a clinician palpation finding sealed from the AI Doctor, who cannot feel the mass.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender bilateral posterior cervical and occipital lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender regional nodes accompany inflammatory scalp infection; a palpation finding not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No neck stiffness, headache, photophobia, drowsiness or confusion (negative deep-infection/CNS screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An alert child with no meningism or neurological features makes deep-space or CNS extension very unlikely and supports outpatient same-day management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (Temp 37.4 C, HR 110, BP 105/65, RR 22, SpO2 99%, Wt 21 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: tinea capitis with kerion, systemically well
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with age-appropriate stable vitals confirms the child is systemically well — a clinician measurement sealed from the AI Doctor, who infers 'systemically well' from the parent-reported absence of fever/lethargy. The weight would be used by the in-person prescriber for dosing; the AI Doctor must not dose.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Patchy scaly alopecia + black-dot broken hairs + a boggy suppurative mass + follicular pustules + a new-puppy vector together define an inflammatory dermatophyte scalp infection needing systemic antifungal therapy and in-person assessment, not incision/drainage.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fungal microscopy and culture of scalp scale / plucked hairs (and Wood's lamp if available) at the in-person visit
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dermatophyte and species, guides duration of systemic therapy; obtained at the in-person assessment, not a telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Incision and drainage of the scalp mass
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The mass is an inflammatory kerion, not a drainable bacterial abscess; incision/drainage is inappropriate, painful and does not treat the fungus. Explicitly to be avoided.
